content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom N:19 (blue sphere) in the center-right portion of the image. Let me carefully identify all atoms directly bonded to N:19 by examining the bond connections:

From the image, I can clearly see that N:19 is connected to:
- C:18 (carbon, yellow/orange sphere) - bonded directly to N:19
- N:15 (nitrogen, blue sphere) - bonded directly to N:19  
- C:20 (carbon, yellow/orange sphere) - bonded directly to N:19

The bonds are clearly visible as connecting lines between these atoms and N:19.

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 19 (N) are:
- Atom index 18, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 15, element N (nitrogen)
- Atom index 20, element C (carbon)

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 737, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_737.xyz".

Therefore, the answer is:
- Neighbors of N:19: [18:C, 15:N, 20:C]
- geomconf_index: 737

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 19, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
